articles.title: Minister seeks to rope in RWAs in sapling plantation drive ‘Bengaluru Hasirooru’ to be launched on August 15
articles.sum: To meet the target, the GBA corporations have collectively earmarked ₹9.64 crore in their budgets, covering both plantation and years of maintenance

articles.title: Krishna Byre Gowda targets ‘paper plantation’ in Bengaluru
articles.sum: According to the Minister, the civic body has not been planting trees as per its claims and has been showing inflated numbers on paper owing to a lack of accountability and improper documentation

articles.title: India summons Russian diplomat over 4 Indians killed in strike off Ukranian coast
articles.sum: Four Indian nationals were killed after a commercial vessel, MV Golden Leo, came under attack while departing Ukraine's port of Odesa on Sunday, the Indian government confirmed. One more Indian crew member remains in critical condition. According to the government, the vessel had...

articles.title: 'Pradhan has to be sacked': Sonam Wangchuk enters Day 24 of hunger strike
articles.sum: Sonam Wangchuk continues his 24th day hunger strike demanding accountability for the NEET paper leak. Protesters resumed their sit-in at Jantar Mantar, pressing for the education minister's resignation. A recent march to Parliament drew significant support from various personalit...

articles.title: Wangchuk continues to refuse IV fluids despite medical advice; low blood sugar, potassium persist
articles.sum: According to the bulletin, Wangchuk remains under the close supervision of a multidisciplinary team of experts from VMMC & Safdarjung Hospital and AIIMS. In view of his ongoing mild to moderate dehydration, persistent laboratory abnormalities and the physiological stress associat...

articles.title: 'Truck carrying Abhijeet Dipke, Gitanjali, Shabana Azmi, Prakash Raj brutally attacked': CJP accuses Delhi Police of violence
articles.sum: The Cockroach Janta Party alleges police brutality during a recent Parliament march. Protesters claim public figures were assaulted and dragged from a truck. One activist's wife reportedly had her hair pulled during the police action. The group states their volunteer suffered sha...

articles.title: Kerala merchant navy man dies in Russian strike off Ukraine ahead of wedding
articles.sum: A 26-year-old merchant navy officer from Kerala died after his ship was hit. The cargo vessel was reportedly struck by Russian forces near Odesa. Akhil Joyan was due to marry in two months and was the sole provider. His family is seeking assistance to bring his body home. India c...

articles.title: 'Bound by shared democratic values': PM Modi congratulates Andy Burnham on becoming UK Prime Minister
articles.sum: Prime Minister Narendra Modi congratulated Andy Burnham on his new role. He expressed confidence that India-UK ties will deepen across key sectors. The two nations share democratic values and cooperate widely in many areas. With CETA entering into force, the partnership is poised...

articles.title: No let-up even after strict punishment for rape: Supreme Court
articles.sum: Awards 20-year jail term to rickshaw puller in 2016 case

articles.title: Supreme Court orders new SIT into Ram temple donation theft, will monitor probe
articles.sum: The Supreme Court has taken over the Ayodhya Ram Mandir fund embezzlement investigation. Local police have been removed from the probe, which will now be led by a senior IPS officer. The Shri Ram Teerth Kshetra Trust must preserve all donation receipts and accounts for scrutiny. ...

articles.title: 70 detained, 118 cops and 60 marchers injured: Police block protesters' march to Parliament; JP Nadda meets CJP representatives
articles.sum: A protest by the Cockroach Janta Party demanding a minister's resignation turned chaotic. Protesters clashed with police, resulting in numerous injuries on both sides. Police reported detentions and filed charges related to rioting and assault on public servants. The BJP blamed L...

articles.title: Police block thousands of 'cockroach' protesters marching on India's parliament
articles.sum: Some protesters demanding education reforms said they were injured in clashes with police who banned the march.

articles.title: From hundreds to thousands: How India's 'cockroach' protest movement has grown
articles.sum: BBC's Azadeh Moshiri reports as thousands attempt to march to India's parliament in support of the Cockroach Janta Party's demand for education reforms.

articles.title: Why are thousands of people protesting in Delhi?
articles.sum: Thousands of mostly young people tried to march on India's parliament to demand changes to how the education system is run.

articles.title: Are England finally improving in ODI cricket?
articles.sum: After beating India 2-1, BBC Sport asks if England are finally building momentum one year out from the World Cup.

articles.title: Diamonds and 'cab to orbit': Rocket aiming to transform India's space ambitions takes off
articles.sum: India's first space-tech unicorn Skyroot Aerospace successfully launched Vikram-1 on Saturday.

articles.title: The hunger-striking engineer demanding education reforms in India
articles.sum: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk has a long history of challenging the status quo and refusing food to highlight his causes.

articles.title: Crackdown on 'objectionable' books in Indian-administered Kashmir sparks row
articles.sum: Authorities in Indian-administered Kashmir have asked educational institutions to review every book on their premises.

articles.title: How India's Lord's win echoes the legacy of its pioneering women cricketers
articles.sum: India won the first women's Test at Lord's, 142 years after its first men's Test.
